title: Arrhenatherum mannii

The genus Arrhenatherum belongs to the subfamily Pooideae, which includes various grass species. The subfamily Pooideae is part of the order Poales, a group of monocotyledonous plants. intro: Arrhenatherum mannii, also known as mann's oat grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of mann's oat grass include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
